items.title: Boras: Tigers ace Skubal could have short rehab
items.summary: Tigers ace Skubal could return sooner than the typical multimonth timeline typical of pitchers who undergo his procedure, according to agent Scott Boras.

items.title: Dodgers get boost with Betts' return set for Mon.
items.summary: Mookie Betts is set to return after missing more than a month because of a right oblique strain when the Dodgers open a four-game series vs. the Giants on Monday.

items.title: Perkins keys win on 1st Mother's Day without mom
items.summary: Milwaukee Brewers outfielder Blake Perkins said he felt "joy" playing for his mom on his first Mother's Day since her death.

items.title: Rangers' deGrom second fastest to 1,900 K's
items.summary: Rangers right-hander Jacob deGrom earned his 1,900th career strikeout in the first inning against the Cubs, becoming the second-fastest pitcher to that milestone by games and innings.

items.title: Tolle honors late mom with gameday attire, song
items.summary: Pitching two years and a day after his mother, Jina, died following a nearly eight-year battle with colon cancer, Payton Tolle honored her by wearing his pant legs near his knees showing his pink socks on Mother's Day.

items.title: Rodón on debut: 'Velo's great,' but not control
items.summary: Carlos Rodón's seven-month recovery from elbow surgery apparently didn't do anything to hinder the Yankees left-hander's velocity. His ability to throw strikes remains a work in progress.

items.title: Red Sox 1B Contreras HBP on hand, leaves game
items.summary: The Red Sox said initial tests on Willson Contreras came back negative after he left Sunday's game against the Rays with a bruised right hand suffered when he was hit by a fastball from Tampa Bay's Nick Martinez.
